display.purpose: Masthead name. One per page, Archivo 900.

headline.purpose: The live-exhibit lead. Sets the proof moment apart.

body.purpose: Taglines, blurbs, prose. Cap measure at 52–66ch.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, LIVE marker, dates, code. JetBrains Mono, uppercase.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Hovered index rows and the decision readout — a slight lift on interaction.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: The elevated specimen card holding the live Cedar exhibit. The page's primary focal lift.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Underline wipes and index-row padding shifts.

components.name: Scenario Chip
components.description: Text-led choice in the live exhibit's scenario picker; active state cues with a magenta border only.

components.name: Numbered Index Row
components.description: The work-list spine: violet ordinal, Archivo-800 title, muted blurb, mono meta. Hairline-separated; lifts and goes magenta on hover.
components.html: 02AVP Access QueriesCedar partial-evaluation residuals turned into indexed lookups.2024 · Case study

components.name: Live Decision Readout
components.description: The 'show, don't tell' centerpiece: an Archivo-900 Allow/Deny word with a mono reason line, on a sunken panel inside the specimen card.

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Mono label, muted at rest; goes Signal Magenta with an underline wiping in on hover.

components.name: Specimen Card
components.description: The elevated container for the live Cedar exhibit. The page's one true elevation (shadow-md).

narrative.overview: An editorial folio — masthead, about, work, experience, colophon — wired like a lab instrument. The structure is print: oversized Archivo headlines, hairline rules, a confident asymmetric masthead. The skin is an instrument: a near-black Dracula surface, monospace readouts, and a single magenta signal that means live. A recruiter reads the folio in ten seconds; an engineer expands a project and runs its live demo in a drawer. The palette is the Dracula theme, chosen because the audience is developers who recognize it on sight. Work and experience are expandable entries; a live project opens its demo in a slide-in drawer, so a new demo is just a new live project — not a new section. It rejects the generic SaaS/portfolio template, costume retro (the retired Win95 desktop), the LinkedIn resume dump, and overdesign for its own sake.
keyCharacteristics: Editorial structure (masthead → about → work → experience → colophon), not a card grid.
Dracula-dark surface; bone-white text; one magenta signal for live.
Archivo display weight 900 paired with JetBrains Mono for every label and number.
Quiet, text-led controls. The interface recedes so the work and the live proof lead.
Depth from layered shadows, not borders-as-decoration.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Magenta means exactly one thing: live / interactive. If an element is not live, hoverable, or the thing that just ran, it is not magenta. Ordinals are violet; success is green; failure is red; everything else is bone or muted.

rules.name: The Lightened-Comment Rule
rules.body: Dracula's comment #6272a4 fails 4.5:1 as body text on the surface black. Body sub-text uses Muted Lavender instead. Comment is reserved for large/bold de-emphasized labels only.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Archivo carries meaning and emotion; JetBrains Mono carries data and machine state. Never set a date, count, ID, or decision in Archivo, and never set a headline in mono.

rules.name: The Heavy-on-Dark Rule
rules.body: Display and headline run at Archivo 900, not 700 — dark backgrounds eat perceived weight, so the masthead must over-commit to read as confident rather than thin.

rules.name: The Deep-Shadow Rule
rules.body: On the Dracula surface, shadows must be near-black and generous (>=10px blur for elevation). A pale, tight shadow reads as a 2014 web app and disappears against the dark.

dos: Do keep Signal Magenta to <=10% of any screen, and only for live / interactive meaning.
Do set every number, date, and machine readout in JetBrains Mono, and every headline in Archivo.
Do run display/headline at Archivo 900 on the dark surface.
Do convey depth with deep, near-black layered shadows (>=10px blur for elevation).
Do keep controls quiet and text-led; let the work and the live proof carry the page.
Do prove ability with working artifacts (the live Cedar exhibit), per show-don't-tell.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS / portfolio template — no gradient hero, no identical icon-card grid, no Inter-everywhere builder look.
Don't reintroduce costume retro — no CRT flicker, beeps, or fake OS windows.
Don't paste a LinkedIn-style resume dump — dense corporate bullet lists with no point of view.
Don't overdesign — no effect that costs clarity or load speed for its own sake.
Don't use gradient text or background-clip: text (single solid colors only).
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right stripe as an accent; borders are hairline and full.
Don't set body sub-text in Dracula Comment #6272a4 — it fails contrast; use Muted Lavender.
Don't let magenta become decoration. If it isn't live, it isn't magenta.
